summary:
The following are the key takeaways from United Airlines's earnings conference call:
- Q3 EPS of $2.78 exceeded both guidance range ($2.25-$2.75) and Wall Street consensus of $2.68
- United expects to be the only airline to grow earnings in 2025, with full-year EPS trending toward the better half of $9-$11 guidance
- S&P upgraded United's credit rating to BB+ from BB on August 12, the highest rating in over two decades
- Premium revenues grew 6% year-over-year with premium cabin PRASM outperforming main cabin by 5 points
- All-time highest business revenue week occurred in October, with 3 of the top 5 best weeks in company history occurring in September 2025
- Loyalty revenues increased 9% YoY, with co-brand remuneration up 15% and expected to end the year up over 12%
- CASM-ex declined 0.9% in Q3, expected to be industry-leading cost performance
- Free cash flow expected to exceed $3 billion this year, with management expecting all 7 hubs were profitable in Q3
- First Starlink-equipped Boeing 737-800 launched with dramatically higher NPS scores described as a 'game changer' for customer experience
- Management targets 1+ point of annual margin expansion, aiming for low-teen margins in current environment and mid-teens as industry restructuring continues
